creator: seth@freebie.dp.ny.frb.org
text:
Sorry to be unclear.  This bug was present in 2.12 and remained in 2.2.
Other people are reporting similar problems with recent builds (of either
nmap or world; I didn't get details).  In their cases, interestingly
enough, there were no ethernet cards... so my workaround didn't help them.
The -S -e workaround DOES help, however... but I wonder what changed.

Thanks,

SB

On Tue, 3 Aug 1999, Kris Kennaway wrote:

> On Fri, 30 Jul 1999, Seth wrote:
> 
> > After the latest build, nmap seems to have broken a bit.  I've isolated it to
> > a case where the box has 2 NICs, and the first one is down AND has never been
> > configured.
> 
> Thanks for the bug report, it may be useful for other people who
> experience this problem and search GNATS for it. However the correct place
> to report it would be to the nmap developers (if I'm understanding
> correctly that this is a bug you're seeing in nmap 2.2 but not in 2.1x)
> 
> Kris
>
